verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 is a lysosomal/late-endosomal ABC transporter with no documented stable plasma membrane localization. It lacks a classical surface receptor architecture, GPI anchor, or cell-state-dependent PM recruitment. No therapeutic programs target it on intact cell surfaces. While a multi-pass TM protein, it resides constitutively in the endomembrane system. Lysosomal exocytosis is theoretically possible but not documented as a primary/physiological route for ABCB9 surface display, and no evidence supports this as therapeutically exploitable.
